title: eriogonum umbellatum var. dumosum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum var. dumosum, also known as the Sulfur Buckwheat or Sulfur Flower Buckwheat, is a flowering plant species in the buckwheat family. It is a perennial shrub with clusters of small, sulfur-yellow flowers and is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum umbellatum var. dumosum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to promote overall health and well-being.
